content:
First we place  
A=(0,0), C=(6,0).  Since AB=4, BC=5, CA=6 we can take  
B = (9/4,\;5√7/4).  
The similarity ABC∼ACD with ratio k=AC/AB=6/4=3/2 and mapping B↦C forces  
D = A + (3/2)·R_{−∠BAB→CAC}(C−A)  
and one finds  
D = (81/16,\;-45√7/16).  

Next P lies on the ray DA beyond A, so P = A + t(A−D).  Imposing ∠BDC=∠ACP one finds the positive solution t=4/9, hence  
P = (−9/4,\;5√7/4).  

Finally  
DP = P−D = (−117/16,\;65√7/16)  
so  
DP² = ((−117)² + (65√7)²)/256 = (13689 +29575)/256 =43264/256 =169.  

Answer: \boxed{169}.

message.content:
First we place  
A=(0,0), C=(6,0).  Since AB=4, BC=5, CA=6 we can take  
B = (9/4,\;5√7/4).  
The similarity ABC∼ACD with ratio k=AC/AB=6/4=3/2 and mapping B↦C forces  
D = A + (3/2)·R_{−∠BAB→CAC}(C−A)  
and one finds  
D = (81/16,\;-45√7/16).  

Next P lies on the ray DA beyond A, so P = A + t(A−D).  Imposing ∠BDC=∠ACP one finds the positive solution t=4/9, hence  
P = (−9/4,\;5√7/4).  

Finally  
DP = P−D = (−117/16,\;65√7/16)  
so  
DP² = ((−117)² + (65√7)²)/256 = (13689 +29575)/256 =43264/256 =169.  

Answer: \boxed{169}.
